steps.title: Separate the two clauses
steps.content: The first clause explains why the Michelin brothers wanted customers to visit recommended hotels and restaurants. The next clause, beginning as it grew in scope and influence, explains how the guide later changed.

steps.title: Use a semicolon boundary
steps.content: A creates a run-on between the clauses, C adds an unnecessary and, and D creates a comma splice. The semicolon cleanly separates the complete thoughts while keeping the related ideas in one sentence. The answer is B.
